page: Amazon Web Services t3a.nano is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t3a server family. It is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz, featuring 1 physical core, 2 threads, and 2 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 0.5 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 0.25 GB of RAM per core. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, does not include local storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of compute and memory capabilities. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests register scores of 577.98 and 589.58 respectively, both falling within the bottom 10% of benchmarked servers. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is measured at 75.16 GB/sec. In database and application tests, the instance achieves 1.24 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 1.58 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation model and highly constrained 0.5 GB memory capacity, this instance is designed for low-resource, intermittent workloads. It is qualitatively highly cost-efficient for basic administrative tasks, light microservices, or low-traffic static web hosting, but is unsuitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, or sustained high-compute workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.nano is a burstable, entry-level instance designed for low-intensity workloads. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz, it features 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core and is equipped with 0.5 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance in the bottom 10%, with stress-ng multi-core scores of 589.58 and a Redis SET throughput of 1.24 million operations per second. Given its highly constrained resource profile and burstable CPU allocation, the t3a.nano is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight utility tasks, small microservices, and basic static web serving, while remaining unsuitable for resource-intensive or sustained database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.nano is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, 2 vCPUs, and 0.5 GB of DDR4 RAM, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic static web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.nano is a burstable instance with an AMD EPYC processor, 2 vCPUs, and 0.5 GB of DDR4 RAM for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A highly constrained, burstable x86_64 instance designed for lightweight utility tasks and low-traffic static web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
Equipped with 0.5 GB of DDR4 system memory and no local storage
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model on the Nitro hypervisor
Benchmark results place compute and memory performance in the bottom 10%
Best suited for low-traffic static web serving and lightweight microservices
